_comment: Wave 2 Item 9.4 — S3 lifecycle policy for the 6 audit tables. Apply via: aws s3api put-bucket-lifecycle-configuration --bucket tickvault-audit --lifecycle-configuration file://deploy/aws/s3-lifecycle-audit-tables.json. Cost target: under 333 INR per month for up to 500 GB across all 6 tables (per .claude/rules/project/aws-budget.md). Tier transitions: Day 0 INTELLIGENT_TIERING, Day 90 GLACIER_IR (Glacier Instant Retrieval — minimum 90d storage), Day 365 DEEP_ARCHIVE (Glacier Deep Archive — minimum 180d storage). 5 of 6 tables expire at 1825 days (5 years). order_audit has NO expiration: SEBI 5y minimum is a floor not a ceiling, and aws-budget.md states 'all orders forever'. Wave-2-D Fix 5 closes the security audit finding that `Expiration: 1825d` would silently delete order audit data exactly at the SEBI floor leaving zero margin for late SEBI inspection requests.

Rules._comment_order_audit: SEBI mandate: 5-year retention is the FLOOR, not a ceiling. aws-budget.md (.claude/rules/project/) states 'all orders forever'. Wave-2-D Fix 5: NO Expiration rule on this prefix — order audit objects are kept indefinitely. Object Lock with COMPLIANCE mode is applied separately via aws s3api put-object-lock-configuration to make these objects immune to early deletion. If storage cost ever needs review, reach for Glacier Deep Archive lifetime retention (~₹0.17/GB-mo at 2026 prices) — never reach for an Expiration rule.
